programmes.show_description: Link REIT: Link Asset Management's Group CEO George Hongchoy opens up in a revealing, candid conversation about the risks, tough calls, and bold bets.
GlobalFoundries: Managing Asia's Christine Tan goes inside into the fast-moving semiconductor world with GlobalFoundries CEO Tim Breen to find out more about the company's bold expansion plans.
When the bell rings, the action does not end. "Closing Bell: Overtime" takes you through all of the after-hours moves including breaking news, corporate earnings and post-market trading.
"Fast Money" is America's post-market show.

Alibaba: Uncover the twists and turns behind Alibaba's rise - from founder Jack Ma's meteoric ascent to how the Chinese tech giant quietly became a leader in artificial intelligence. (Ep1)
Nintendo: Enter the Nintendo universe. Discover how the former toymaker turned plumbers and princesses into a gaming empire, and why its bet on movies and theme parks might be its biggest gamble yet.
Ping An: In the next episode of Managing Asia, Christine Tan heads to Shenzhen to sit down with Michael Guo, Co-CEO of Ping An Group. (Ep970)
Levi Strauss & Co: Michelle Gass, Levi Strauss & Co's first female CEO in 170 years, speaks with Christine Tan in an exclusive interview in Tokyo.
Inside Popmart: CNBC takes viewers behind the scenes of China's booming IP powerhouse as it looks beyond its home market for its next wave of growth.
Inside Sats: On the next On Assignment, CNBC explores how Singapore-based SATS rose from a regional inflight catering provider to the world's largest air cargo handler. (Ep43)
Pinterest's Bill Ready: Pinterest CEO Bill Ready reflects on the pivotal decisions behind his rise from small-town- Kentucky to Silicon Valley.

Philips' Roy Jakobs: Shaped by early lessons in forgiveness and resilience, Roy Jakobs shares how moments of discomfort, and compassion, prepared him for the Philips CEO role.
Can Policy Create Prosperity? Hangzhou's Bet, France's Test: This month's edition explores why Hangzhou has become a breeding ground for some of China's most promising tech startups.
Paris Hilton: Paris Hilton, often called the "original influencer," takes Tania Bryer to a New York hotel steeped in family history and shares how she turned pain into purpose.
